Metadata.title: Nam's Portfolio | Backend Developer
Metadata.description: Backend Developer specializing in high-performance game server architecture and real-time systems. Explore my projects and technical expertise.

Hero.headline: I design backend systems that stay fast under multiplayer and real-time pressure.
Hero.summary: Backend Developer focused on game server architecture, low-latency messaging, and data paths that hold up under concurrent load.

proofItems.scale: Systems built for thousands of concurrent players
proofItems.availability: Available for freelance and consulting work

runtime.title: Runtime stability first
runtime.description: Designing event flow and service behavior to stay predictable under real-time pressure.

observability.title: Observability built in
observability.description: Logging, alerting, and operational signals are treated as part of the product, not afterthoughts.

delivery.title: Delivery under constraints
delivery.description: Shipping features while protecting throughput, debugging speed, and system clarity.

About.title: About
About.summaryTitle: Backend engineer with a systems-first mindset
About.summary: I work on backend systems where latency, throughput, and operational visibility directly affect player experience. My strength is turning product requirements into stable runtime behavior, cleaner observability, and infrastructure that remains understandable as it scales.
About.positioning: Best fit for teams building multiplayer, event-driven, or performance-sensitive products.

Skills.title: Capabilities
Skills.intro: The stack matters, but the value is how each layer helps me build fast, stable, and observable systems.

Descriptions.backend: API design, runtime structure, and service boundaries that stay maintainable as product scope grows.
Descriptions.realtime: Connection handling, event flow, and low-latency messaging for interactive systems.
Descriptions.database: Query design, caching strategy, and data access patterns tuned for throughput and response time.
Descriptions.frontend: Enough frontend depth to ship product surfaces, internal tools, and technical demos without handoff friction.
Descriptions.devops: Daily workflow, deployment hygiene, and operational tooling that keep development and debugging efficient.

Experience.title: Experience
Experience.intro: The strongest signal in this portfolio is shipped backend work under live product constraints.

Projects.title: Selected Projects
Projects.intro: A small set of projects chosen to show architecture decisions, delivery ownership, and technical judgment.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.lead: If you are hiring for backend systems, real-time infrastructure, or performance-focused product work, I am open to a focused conversation.
Contact.directDescription: Email or LinkedIn is the fastest path. The form is available if you prefer async outreach.
Contact.formDescription: Useful for project briefs, collaboration inquiries, or detailed context.
Contact.name: Name
Contact.subject: Subject
Contact.success: Message sent! I'll get back to you soon.
Contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
Contact.availabilityValue: Open to selected freelance, consulting, and recruiter conversations.

Profile.name: Nam
Profile.title: Backend Developer
Profile.tagLine: Specializing in game server architecture, real-time systems, and high-throughput backend engineering.
Profile.availability: Open to freelance projects and consulting opportunities.
Profile.description: Backend Developer with 2+ years of hands-on experience architecting and optimizing game server infrastructure. I build scalable, low-latency systems for products where concurrency, uptime, and response time are not optional.
Profile.contactDescription: Strong fit for teams building multiplayer products, event-driven backends, or systems that need better runtime performance and observability.
highlights: Design backend flows that remain understandable under live product complexity.
Optimize event, socket, and data paths for lower latency and less operational friction.
Bridge implementation and debugging with stronger logging, monitoring, and runtime visibility.

onky.title: Backend Developer (Game Server)
onky.description: Owned backend architecture and runtime stability for multiple online game products, covering server structure, real-time event handling, data performance, and production observability.
onky.scope: Architect and maintain game server infrastructure, optimize live systems, and support feature delivery under multiplayer and operational constraints.
onky.impact: Improved throughput, debugging speed, and system visibility while supporting thousands of concurrent players across production game servers.
achievements: Deployed 20+ game server instances that supported thousands of concurrent players across live products.
Built custom event systems into the core server architecture to support product-specific gameplay logic.
Created an S.O.S monitoring pipeline for real-time alerting, log aggregation, and automated reporting across Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
Reduced database queries by 40% through random distribution algorithm optimization, improving response latency on critical flows.
Refactored the logging layer to enable more granular debugging and faster issue isolation in production.
systems: Real-time gameplay events and socket communication
Game server deployment and runtime orchestration
Database performance tuning for high-traffic workloads

vmu.title: VMU Test Exam
vmu.description: Online exam platform for medical students with real-time admin visibility, question bank management, and exam session support.
vmu.problem: The product needed a stable assessment flow for students plus a moderation layer for admins to monitor live sessions.
vmu.role: Built backend-heavy product flows and real-time session management between user activity and admin tooling.
vmu.impact: Delivered a production-ready learning platform with real-time moderation support and clearer operational visibility during exam activity.

tinylink.title: TinyLink
tinylink.description: URL shortener built as a lean systems exercise around performance, reliability, and observability on constrained infrastructure.
tinylink.problem: Most URL shorteners hide the engineering tradeoffs. This project was built to explore how far a compact service can go with careful runtime and data-path decisions.
tinylink.role: Designed and implemented the service architecture, persistence layer, cache strategy, and deployment path.
tinylink.impact: Showcases engineering judgment around fast redirects, service reliability, and observability in a cost-sensitive environment.

Footer.tagline: Backend developer focused on real-time systems and scalable infrastructure.
